devanagari: मैत्रेय उवाच । एवं आविष्कृताशेष गुणकर्मोदयो मुनिम् । सव्रीड इव तं सम्राड् उपारतमुवाच ह ॥ १ ॥
english_devnagari: maitreya uvāca evaṃ āviṣkṛtāśeṣa guṇakarmodayo munim savrīḍa iva taṃ samrāḍ upāratamuvāca ha

analyses.morphological_and_syntactical_analysis: [perfect active first single], [perfect active third single]
[perfect active first single], [perfect active third single]
[perfect active first single], [perfect active third single]
[perfect active first single], [perfect active third single]

translation:
Maitreya said:
The emperor (Manu) whose all excellent attributes and deeds were thus eulogised, spoke rather bashfully (due to his modesty at hearing his own praise or the fear of the rejection of the marriage proposal) to the sage full of quietism (and dissociation with worldly acts).

devanagari: मनुरुवाच । ब्रह्मासृजत्स्वमुखतो युष्मान् आत्मपरीप्सया । छन्दोमयस्तपोविद्या योगयुक्तानलम्पटान् ॥ २ ॥
english_devnagari: manuruvāca brahmāsṛjatsvamukhato yuṣmān ātmaparīpsayā chandomayastapovidyā yogayuktānalampaṭān

analyses.morphological_and_syntactical_analysis: [perfect active first single], [perfect active third single]
[perfect active first single], [perfect active third single]

translation:
Manu said:[1]
With a desire to preserve himself, Brahmā who is the Veda incarnate, created from his mouth you Brāhmaṇas who are full of tapas, learning, yoga and are free from lust.[2]
purport:
Footnote [1]:
Verses 2 and 3 are the echo of the Puruṣa-sūkta.

Footnote [2]:
Tapo-vidyā...yuktān: (i) Possessed karma upāsanā (devotion) and jñāna (knowledge)—Bhāgavata Candrikā (ii) Full of tapas, knowledge of śāstras and devotion (bhakti)—Padaratnāvalī

devanagari: तत्त्राणायासृजत् चास्मान् दोः सहस्रात्सहस्रपात् । हृदयं तस्य हि ब्रह्म क्षत्रमङ्गं प्रचक्षते ॥ ३ ॥
english_devnagari: tattrāṇāyāsṛjat cāsmān doḥ sahasrātsahasrapāt hṛdayaṃ tasya hi brahma kṣatramaṅgaṃ pracakṣate
translation: The thousand-legged God (Brahmā) created us from bis thousand arms for their (Brāhmaṇas’) protection. It is said that Brāhmaṇas form his heart and Kṣatriyas his body (limbs).

devanagari: अतो ह्यन्योन्यमात्मानं ब्रह्म क्षत्रं च रक्षतः । रक्षति स्माव्ययो देवः स यः सदसदात्मकः ॥ ४ ॥
english_devnagari: ato hyanyonyamātmānaṃ brahma kṣatraṃ ca rakṣataḥ rakṣati smāvyayo devaḥ sa yaḥ sadasadātmakaḥ

analyses.morphological_and_syntactical_analysis: [accusative plural from √ rakṣ class 1 verb], [ablative single from √ rakṣ class 1 verb], [genitive single from √ rakṣ class 1 verb]
[ablative single from √ rakṣ class 1 verb], [genitive single from √ rakṣ class 1 verb]

translation: Hence Brāhmaṇas and Kṣatriyas protect each other. The immutable, disinterested God who is the Soul (antaryāmin) of all[3] thus protects all.
purport:
Footnote [3]:
sadasadātmakaḥ [sadasadātmaka]: (i) Whose nature is of causal relation, lit. who is of the form of cause and effect—Bhāgavata Candrikā
(ii) Who is comprised of cause and effect.

devanagari: तव सन्दर्शनादेव च्छिन्ना मे सर्वसंशयाः । यत्स्वयं भगवान् प्रीत्या धर्ममाह रिरक्षिषोः ॥ ५ ॥
english_devnagari: tava sandarśanādeva cchinnā me sarvasaṃśayāḥ yatsvayaṃ bhagavān prītyā dharmamāha rirakṣiṣoḥ
translation: All my doubts have been resolved by your very sight as Your worship himself has explained to me, out of favour, the duty (dharma) of one who desires to protect (the world).

devanagari: दिष्ट्या मे भगवान् दृष्टो दुर्दर्शो योऽकृतात्मनाम् । दिष्ट्या पादरजः स्पृष्टं शीर्ष्णा मे भवतः शिवम् ॥ ६ ॥
english_devnagari: diṣṭyā me bhagavān dṛṣṭo durdarśo yo diṣṭyā pādarajaḥ spṛṣṭaṃ śīrṣṇā me bhavataḥ śivam

analyses.morphological_and_syntactical_analysis: [nominative single from √ spṛś class 6 verb], [accusative single from √ spṛś class 6 verb]

translation: It is my good luck that I could see your revered self who are difficult to be met by those who have not controlled their minds. I am happy that I could touch the holy dust of your honour’s feet with my head.

devanagari: दिष्ट्या त्वयानुशिष्टोऽहं कृतश्चानुग्रहो महान् । अपावृतैः कर्णरन्ध्रैः जुष्टा दिष्ट्योशतीर्गिरः ॥ ७ ॥
english_devnagari: diṣṭyā tvayānuśiṣṭo apāvṛtaiḥ karṇarandhraiḥ juṣṭā diṣṭyośatīrgiraḥ

analyses.morphological_and_syntactical_analysis: [nominative single from √ kṛ class 1 verb], [nominative single from √ kṛ class 2 verb], [nominative single from √ kṛ class 5 verb], [nominative single from √ kṛ class 8 verb]

[nominative plural from √ juṣ class 1 verb], [vocative plural from √ juṣ class 1 verb], [nominative plural from √ juṣ class 3 verb], [vocative plural from √ juṣ class 3 verb], [nominative plural from √ juṣ class 6 verb], [vocative plural from √ juṣ class 6 verb]

[nominative plural from √ juṣ class 1 verb], [vocative plural from √ juṣ class 1 verb], [accusative plural from √ juṣ class 1 verb], [nominative plural from √ juṣ class 3 verb], [vocative plural from √ juṣ class 3 verb], [accusative plural from √ juṣ class 3 verb], [nominative plural from √ juṣ class 6 verb], [vocative plural from √ juṣ class 6 verb], [accusative plural from √ juṣ class 6 verb]

translation: It is a great fortune that I have been advised (taught) by you. A great favour has been done to me. Your sweet words have been luckily received by my open ears (i.e. I could eagerly hear etc).

devanagari: स भवान् दुहितृस्नेह परिक्लिष्टात्मनो मम । श्रोतुमर्हसि दीनस्य श्रावितं कृपया मुने ॥ ८ ॥
english_devnagari: sa bhavān duhitṛsneha parikliṣṭātmano mama śrotumarhasi dīnasya śrāvitaṃ kṛpayā mune

analyses.morphological_and_syntactical_analysis: [nominative single from √ śru ], [accusative single from √ śru ]

translation: Oh sage, your worship should kindly listen to the request of a distressed person like me whose mind is tormented by affection for his own daughter.

devanagari: प्रियव्रतोत्तानपदोः स्वसेयं दुहिता मम । अन्विच्छति पतिं युक्तं वयःशीलगुणादिभिः ॥ ९ ॥
english_devnagari: priyavratottānapadoḥ svaseyaṃ duhitā mama anvicchati patiṃ yuktaṃ vayaḥśīlaguṇādibhiḥ

analyses.morphological_and_syntactical_analysis: [nominative single from √ yuj class 7 verb], [accusative single from √ yuj class 7 verb]

translation: This daughter of mine is the sister of Priyavrata and Uttānapāda. She desires to marry a husband who is suitable to her in age. character, excellences and other attributes.

devanagari: यदा तु भवतः शील श्रुतरूपवयोगुणान् । अशृणोत् नारदाद् एषा त्वय्यासीत् कृतनिश्चया ॥ १० ॥
english_devnagari: yadā tu bhavataḥ śīla śrutarūpavayoguṇān aśṛṇot nāradād eṣā tvayyāsīt kṛtaniścayā

analyses.morphological_and_syntactical_analysis: [aorist active third single], [injunctive active third single]

translation: When this (daughter) had heard from Nārada Your honour’s excellent character, learning, beauty, youth and qualities, she has firmly decided to marry you.

devanagari: तत्प्रतीच्छ द्विजाग्र्येमां श्रद्धयोपहृतां मया । सर्वात्मनानुरूपां ते गृहमेधिषु कर्मसु ॥ ११ ॥
english_devnagari: tatpratīccha dvijāgryemāṃ śraddhayopahṛtāṃ mayā sarvātmanānurūpāṃ te gṛhamedhiṣu karmasu
translation: Oh eminent Brāhmaṇa, therefore accept this (daughter) who has been respectfully offered by me. She is in all respects suitable to you in carrying out sacrificial and other duties as a householder.

devanagari: उद्यतस्य हि कामस्य प्रतिवादो न शस्यते । अपि निर्मुक्तसङ्गस्य कामरक्तस्य किं पुनः ॥ १२ ॥
english_devnagari: udyatasya hi kāmasya prativādo na śasyate api nirmuktasaṅgasya kāmaraktasya kiṃ punaḥ

analyses.morphological_and_syntactical_analysis: [genitive single from √ raj class 1 verb], [genitive single from √ raj class 4 verb]
[genitive single from √ raj class 1 verb], [genitive single from √ raj class 4 verb]
[genitive single from √ rañj class 1 verb], [genitive single from √ rañj class 4 verb]
[genitive single from √ rañj class 1 verb], [genitive single from √ rañj class 4 verb]

translation: It is not commendable even for a person who has given up attachment, to reject a desired object when it offers itself. What need be said in the case of a person who is attached to worldly objects?

devanagari: य उद्यतमनादृत्य कीनाशं अभियाचते । क्षीयते तद्यशः स्फीतं मानश्चावज्ञया हतः ॥ १३ ॥
english_devnagari: ya udyatamanādṛtya kīnāśaṃ abhiyācate kṣīyate tadyaśaḥ sphītaṃ mānaścāvajñayā hataḥ

analyses.morphological_and_syntactical_analysis: [nominative single from √ sphā class 1 verb], [accusative single from √ sphā class 1 verb]
[nominative single from √ man class 4 verb], [nominative single from √ man class 8 verb]
[nominative single from √ han class 1 verb], [nominative single from √ han class 2 verb]

translation: A person who, having disrespected an offer (of a desired object) begs the same of a miser, gets (finds) his widespread fame diminished and his self-respect ruined by disrespect (from others).

devanagari: अहं त्वाश्रृणवं विद्वन् विवाहार्थं समुद्यतम् । अतस्त्वं उपकुर्वाणः प्रत्तां प्रतिगृहाण मे ॥ १४ ॥
english_devnagari: ahaṃ tvāśrṛṇavaṃ vidvan vivāhārthaṃ samudyatam atastvaṃ upakurvāṇaḥ prattāṃ pratigṛhāṇa me
translation: Oh learned one, I have heard that you are ready to get yourself married. As you are to terminate your period of celebacy, you please accept (my daughter) offered to you.

devanagari: ऋषिरुवाच । बाढमुद्वोढुकामोऽहं अप्रत्ता च तवात्मजा । आवयोः अनुरूपोऽसौ आद्यो वैवाहिको विधिः ॥ १५ ॥
english_devnagari: ṛṣiruvāca bāḍhamudvoḍhukāmo āvayoḥ anurūpo

analyses.morphological_and_syntactical_analysis: [perfect active first single], [perfect active third single]
[perfect active first single], [perfect active third single]

translation:
The Sage said:
I definitely wish to marry. Your daughter is also not proposed to any other person. This first (or important) union in marriage of ours is suitable.

devanagari: कामः स भूयान् नरदेव तेऽस्याः । पुत्र्याः समाम्नायविधौ प्रतीतः । क एव ते तनयां नाद्रियेत । स्वयैव कान्त्या क्षिपतीमिव श्रियम् ॥ १६ ॥
english_devnagari: kāmaḥ sa bhūyān naradeva te putryāḥ samāmnāyavidhau pratītaḥ ka eva te tanayāṃ nādriyeta svayaiva kāntyā kṣipatīmiva śriyam

analyses.morphological_and_syntactical_analysis: [nominative dual from √ kṣip class 6 verb], [vocative dual from √ kṣip class 6 verb], [accusative dual from √ kṣip class 6 verb], [locative single from √ kṣip class 6 verb]

translation: Oh King, may the desire (of procreation) expressed in the mantras of Vedic marriage-ceremony be fulfilled by my marriage with your daughter. Who will not feel respect for your daughter who surpasses ornaments by her complexion?

devanagari: यां हर्म्यपृष्ठे क्वणदङ्घ्रिशोभां । विक्रीडतीं कन्दुकविह्वलाक्षीम् । विश्वावसुर्न्यपतत् स्वात् विमानात् । विलोक्य सम्मोहविमूढचेताः ॥ १७...
english_devnagari: yāṃ harmyapṛṣṭhe kvaṇadaṅghriśobhāṃ vikrīḍatīṃ kandukavihvalākṣīm viśvāvasurnyapatat svāt vimānāt vilokya sammohavimūḍhacetāḥ

analyses.morphological_and_syntactical_analysis: [nominative single from √ kvaṇ class 1 verb], [vocative single from √ kvaṇ class 1 verb], [accusative single from √ kvaṇ class 1 verb]

translation: (Who would not like to marry your daughter) on seeing whom playing (with a ball) on the terrace of your palace and looking with a perturbed glance at the ball and her feet beautified by tinkling anklets, (Gandharva) Viśvāvasu got his mind bewildered by infatuation (made love for her) and fell down from his aerial car.

devanagari: तां प्रार्थयन्तीं ललनाललामं । असेवितश्रीचरणैरदृष्टाम् । वत्सां मनोरुच्चपदः स्वसारं । को नानुमन्येत बुधोऽभियाताम् ॥ १८ ॥
english_devnagari: tāṃ prārthayantīṃ lalanālalāmaṃ asevitaśrīcaraṇairadṛṣṭām vatsāṃ manoruccapadaḥ svasāraṃ ko nānumanyeta budho

analyses.morphological_and_syntactical_analysis: [nominative dual], [nominative plural], [vocative dual], [vocative plural], [accusative dual], [accusative plural]

[nominative dual from √ i class 2 verb], [nominative plural from √ i class 2 verb], [vocative dual from √ i class 2 verb], [vocative plural from √ i class 2 verb], [accusative dual from √ i class 2 verb], [accusative plural from √ i class 2 verb]
[optative middle third single], [optative passive third single]

translation: When Manu's daughter and Uttānapāda’s sister who is the ornament of beautiful women, and who cannot be even seen by persons who have not served the feet of Lakṣmī, approaches with a request for marriage, what wise man will not give his consent?

devanagari: अतो भजिष्ये समयेन साध्वीं । यावत्तेजो बिभृयाद् आत्मनो मे । अतो धर्मान् पारमहंस्यमुख्यान् । शुक्लप्रोक्तान् बहु मन्येऽविहिंस्रान् ॥ १९ ॥
english_devnagari: ato bhajiṣye samayena sādhvīṃ yāvattejo bibhṛyād ātmano me ato dharmān pāramahaṃsyamukhyān śuklaproktān bahu manye

analyses.morphological_and_syntactical_analysis: [present middle first single], [present passive first single]

translation: Hence I will accept this pious (daughter of yours) on condition of staying with her till she bears a child to me. Thereafter I shall think more of the duties as taught by Viṣṇu (such as śama, dama) essential for attaining knowledge and which are characterised by ahiṃsā.

devanagari: यतोऽभवद्विश्वमिदं विचित्रं । संस्थास्यते यत्र च वावतिष्ठते । प्रजापतीनां पतिरेष मह्यं । परं प्रमाणं भगवान् अनन्तः ॥ २० ॥
english_devnagari: yato saṃsthāsyate yatra ca vāvatiṣṭhate prajāpatīnāṃ patireṣa mahyaṃ paraṃ pramāṇaṃ bhagavān anantaḥ

analyses.morphological_and_syntactical_analysis: [accusative plural from √ i class 2 verb], [ablative single from √ i class 2 verb], [genitive single from √ i class 2 verb]

translation: That infinite Eternal Lord, the Lord of all Prajāpatis is the highest authority to me—the Lord from whom was evolved this wonderful diversified universe, in whom it is sustained and in whom it will get dissolved.

devanagari: मैत्रेय उवाच । स उग्रधन्वन् इयदेवाबभाषे । आसीच्च तूष्णीं अरविन्दनाभम् । धियोपगृह्णन् स्मितशोभितेन । मुखेन चेतो लुलुभे देवहूत्याः ॥ २१ ॥
english_devnagari: maitreya uvāca sa ugradhanvan iyadevābabhāṣe āsīcca tūṣṇīṃ aravindanābham dhiyopagṛhṇan smitaśobhitena mukhena ceto lulubhe devahūtyāḥ

[perfect active first single], [perfect active third single]

[aorist active third single], [injunctive active third single]
[nominative single from √ grah class 9 verb], [vocative single from √ grah class 9 verb]

translation:
Maitreya said:
Oh Vidura (wielder of a terrible bow), having spoken this much, the sage became silent quietly meditating over Lord Viṣṇu from whose navel has sprouted forth a lotus. But the heart of Devahūti was captivated by his smiling face (or he enticed Devahūti’s heart by his smiling countenance).

devanagari: सोऽनु ज्ञात्वा व्यवसितं महिष्या दुहितुः स्फुटम् । तस्मै गुणगणाढ्याय ददौ तुल्यां प्रहर्षितः ॥ २२ ॥
english_devnagari: so tasmai guṇagaṇāḍhyāya dadau tulyāṃ praharṣitaḥ

analyses.morphological_and_syntactical_analysis: [perfect active first single], [perfect active third single]
[perfect active first single], [perfect active third single]
[perfect active first single], [perfect active third single]
[perfect active first single], [perfect active third single]
[perfect active first single], [perfect active third single]
[perfect active first single], [perfect active third single]

translation: Having learnt the firm resolution of the queen and his daughter, he was overjoyed and gave in marriage to the sage who was endowed with excellent qualities, his daughter who was equal to him.

devanagari: शतरूपा महाराज्ञी पारिबर्हान् महाधनान् । दम्पत्योः पर्यदात् प्रीत्या भूषावासः परिच्छदान् ॥ २३ ॥
english_devnagari: śatarūpā mahārājñī pāribarhān mahādhanān dampatyoḥ paryadāt prītyā bhūṣāvāsaḥ paricchadān

analyses.morphological_and_syntactical_analysis: [imperfect active third single], [aorist active third single]

translation: Empress Śatarūpā, out of affection bestowed upon the couple very costly marriage gifts such as ornaments, garments and articles of household use.

devanagari: प्रत्तां दुहितरं सम्राट् सदृक्षाय गतव्यथः । उपगुह्य च बाहुभ्यां औत्कण्ठ्योन्मथिताशयः ॥ २४ ॥
english_devnagari: prattāṃ duhitaraṃ samrāṭ sadṛkṣāya gatavyathaḥ upaguhya ca bāhubhyāṃ autkaṇṭhyonmathitāśayaḥ
translation: The King felt free from anxiety at the marriage of his daughter with a suitable bridegroom. He whose heart was greatly agitated by sorrow (due to the prospective departure of his daughter) took her in his arms.

devanagari: अशक्नुवन् तद्विरहं मुञ्चन् बाष्पकलां मुहुः । आसिञ्चद् अम्ब वत्सेति नेत्रोदैर्दुहितुः शिखाः ॥ २५ ॥
english_devnagari: aśaknuvan tadvirahaṃ muñcan bāṣpakalāṃ muhuḥ āsiñcad amba vatseti netrodairduhituḥ śikhāḥ

analyses.morphological_and_syntactical_analysis: [nominative single from √ muc class 6 verb], [vocative single from √ muc class 6 verb]
[nominative single from √ muñc class 1 verb], [vocative single from √ muñc class 1 verb]

translation: Being unable to bear separation from her, he constantly shed tears. Calling her ‘Oh dear, Oh child’, he drenched the hair of his daughter with his tears.

devanagari: आमन्त्र्य तं मुनिवरं अनुज्ञातः सहानुगः । प्रतस्थे रथमारुह्य सभार्यः स्वपुरं नृपः ॥ २६ ॥
english_devnagari: āmantrya taṃ munivaraṃ anujñātaḥ sahānugaḥ pratasthe rathamāruhya sabhāryaḥ svapuraṃ nṛpaḥ
translation: He took leave of the eminent sage. Being permitted by him, he got into the chariot with his wife and set out to his capital along with his retinue.

devanagari: उभयोः ऋषिकुल्यायाः सरस्वत्याः सुरोधसोः । ऋषीणां उपशान्तानां पश्यन् आश्रमसम्पदः ॥ २७ ॥
english_devnagari: ubhayoḥ ṛṣikulyāyāḥ sarasvatyāḥ surodhasoḥ ṛṣīṇāṃ upaśāntānāṃ paśyan āśramasampadaḥ
translation: (On way) he saw the beautiful hermitages of tranquil-minded sages on both the beautiful banks of the sacred river Sarasvatī.

devanagari: तं आयान्तं अभिप्रेत्य ब्रह्मावर्तात्प्रजाः पतिम् । गीतसंस्तुतिवादित्रैः प्रत्युदीयुः प्रहर्षिताः ॥ २८ ॥
english_devnagari: taṃ āyāntaṃ abhipretya brahmāvartātprajāḥ patim gītasaṃstutivāditraiḥ pratyudīyuḥ praharṣitāḥ
translation: Hearing that the king was returning, his subjects from Brahmāvarta became delighted, and came forward to greet him by songs, eulogies and playing on musical instruments.

devanagari: बर्हिष्मती नाम पुरी सर्वसंपत् समन्विता । न्यपतन्यत्र रोमाणि यज्ञस्याङ्गं विधुन्वतः ॥ २९ ॥
english_devnagari: barhiṣmatī nāma purī sarvasaṃpat samanvitā nyapatanyatra romāṇi yajñasyāṅgaṃ vidhunvataḥ
translation: (The place) where the hair of the Yajña Varāha fell while he shook his body, there arose the town called Barhiṣmatī endowed with all kinds of riches and prosperity.

devanagari: कुशाः काशास्त एवासन् शश्वद्धरितवर्चसः । ऋषयो यैः पराभाव्य यज्ञघ्नान् यज्ञमीजिरे ॥ ३० ॥
english_devnagari: kuśāḥ kāśāsta evāsan śaśvaddharitavarcasaḥ ṛṣayo yaiḥ parābhāvya yajñaghnān yajñamījire
translation: Those hair became the ever-green kuśa and kāsa with which the sages defeated the trouble-makers of sacrifices and performed sacrifice, (worshipped Yajña-Viṣṇu).

devanagari: कुशकाशमयं बर्हिः आस्तीर्य भगवान् मनुः । अयजद् यज्ञपुरुषं लब्धा स्थानं यतो भुवम् ॥ ३१ ॥
english_devnagari: kuśakāśamayaṃ barhiḥ āstīrya bhagavān manuḥ ayajad yajñapuruṣaṃ labdhā sthānaṃ yato bhuvam

analyses.morphological_and_syntactical_analysis: [accusative plural from √ i class 2 verb], [ablative single from √ i class 2 verb], [genitive single from √ i class 2 verb]
[ablative single from √ i class 2 verb], [genitive single from √ i class 2 verb]

translation: Having spread out a layer (—seat) of Kuśa and Kāśa grass, revered Manu performed sacrifice for Viṣṇu (Yajñapuruṣa) and the earth as his place of residence from him.

devanagari: बर्हिष्मतीं नाम विभुः यां निर्विश्य समावसत् । तस्यां प्रविष्टो भवनं तापत्रयविनाशनम् ॥ ३२ ॥
english_devnagari: barhiṣmatīṃ nāma vibhuḥ yāṃ nirviśya samāvasat tasyāṃ praviṣṭo bhavanaṃ tāpatrayavināśanam
translation: The king went into (his capital) Barhiṣmatī wherein he lived. Therein he entered his palace which (is free from) the three types of afflictions (viz. ādhibhautika, ādhyātmika, ādhidaivika).

devanagari: सभार्यः सप्रजः कामान् बुभुजेऽन्याविरोधतः । सङ्गीयमानसत्कीर्तिः सस्त्रीभिः सुरगायकैः । प्रत्यूषेष्वनुबद्धेन हृदा श्रृण्वन् हरेः कथाः ॥ ३...
english_devnagari: sabhāryaḥ saprajaḥ kāmān bubhuje saṅgīyamānasatkīrtiḥ sastrībhiḥ suragāyakaiḥ pratyūṣeṣvanubaddhena hṛdā śrṛṇvan hareḥ kathāḥ

analyses.morphological_and_syntactical_analysis: [perfect middle first single], [perfect middle third single]
[perfect middle first single], [perfect middle third single]

translation: With his wife and along with his subjects, be enjoyed pleasures without conflicting other puruṣārthas (viz. dharma, artha and mokṣa). His glorious fame was being sung by heavenly musicians along with their wives. Every day, at dawn, he heard the stories of Hari with a devoted heart

devanagari: निष्णातं योगमायासु मुनिं स्वायम्भुवं मनुम् । यदाभ्रंशयितुं भोगा न शेकुर्भगवत्परम् ॥ ३४ ॥
english_devnagari: niṣṇātaṃ yogamāyāsu muniṃ svāyambhuvaṃ manum yadābhraṃśayituṃ bhogā na śekurbhagavatparam
translation: Pleasures could not swerve the sage Svāyambhuva Manu (from the path of dharma) to the slightest degree as he was a past-master in yoga-māyā (and hence could create his desired objects), and was intensely devoted to Viṣṇu.

devanagari: अयातयामाः तस्यासन् यामाः स्वान्तरयापनाः । श्रृण्वतो ध्यायतो विष्णोः कुर्वतो ब्रुवतः कथाः ॥ ३५ ॥
english_devnagari: ayātayāmāḥ tasyāsan yāmāḥ svāntarayāpanāḥ śrṛṇvato dhyāyato viṣṇoḥ kurvato bruvataḥ kathāḥ

analyses.morphological_and_syntactical_analysis: [accusative plural from √ kṛ class 8 verb], [ablative single from √ kṛ class 8 verb], [genitive single from √ kṛ class 8 verb]
[ablative single from √ kṛ class 8 verb], [genitive single from √ kṛ class 8 verb]

translation: As he was (always busy in) listening to, meditating over, composing and describing the stories of Viṣṇu, (even) the small units of time during the period allotted to him (called Manvantara) were not spent unfruitfully.

devanagari: स एवं स्वान्तरं निन्ये युगानां एकसप्ततिम् । वासुदेवप्रसङ्गेन परिभूतगतित्रयः ॥ ३६ ॥
english_devnagari: sa evaṃ svāntaraṃ ninye yugānāṃ ekasaptatim vāsudevaprasaṅgena paribhūtagatitrayaḥ

analyses.morphological_and_syntactical_analysis: [perfect middle first single], [perfect middle third single]

translation: In this way, he passed his prescribed period (called Manvantara) consisting of seventy-one Yugas. He overpowered (counteracted) the influence of three gatis (viz. weals or woes of the ādhyātmika, ādhibhautika and ādhidaivika types), by his devotion to Vāsudeva.

devanagari: शारीरा मानसा दिव्या वैयासे ये च मानुषाः । भौतिकाश्च कथं क्लेशा बाधन्ते हरिसंश्रयम् ॥ ३७ ॥
english_devnagari: śārīrā mānasā divyā vaiyāse ye ca mānuṣāḥ bhautikāśca kathaṃ kleśā bādhante harisaṃśrayam
translation: Oh Vidura, how can afflictions of a physical and psychological nature or sufferings due to heavenly or human cause affect one who has resorted to Hari?

devanagari: यः पृष्टो मुनिभिः प्राह धर्मान् नानाविधान् शुभान् । नृणां वर्णाश्रमाणां च सर्वभूतहितः सदा ॥ ३८ ॥
english_devnagari: yaḥ pṛṣṭo munibhiḥ prāha dharmān nānāvidhān śubhān nṛṇāṃ varṇāśramāṇāṃ ca sarvabhūtahitaḥ sadā
translation: He was always obliging to all beings. When consulted by the sages, he explained to them the various auspicious paths of duties prescribed for men belonging to all Varṇas (classes of people) and Aśramas (stages in life.).

devanagari: एतत्ते आदिराजस्य मनोश्चरितमद्‍भुतम् । वर्णितं वर्णनीयस्य तदपत्योदयं श्रृणु ॥ ३९ ॥
english_devnagari: etatte ādirājasya manoścaritamad‍bhutam varṇitaṃ varṇanīyasya tadapatyodayaṃ śrṛṇu

analyses.morphological_and_syntactical_analysis: [nominative single from √ varṇ class 10 verb], [accusative single from √ varṇ class 10 verb]

translation:
This wonderful life of the ancient (first) praiseworthy king Manu has been described to you. Now listen to the great fortune of his daughter (Devahūti).
[back to top]
